frame_analyses.description: Black frame - video not yet started
Right needle driver grasping curved needle near tip, left grasper visible

frame_analyses.technique_notes: Fine adjustment of needle position, proper two-handed coordination

frame_analyses.description: Beginning intracorporeal knot tying with suture material visible
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Suture appears to be silk material, appropriate length visible

frame_analyses.description: Active knot tying in progress, instruments crossed
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Proper crossing technique for intracorporeal knot formation

frame_analyses.description: Continued knot manipulation, suture being wrapped around instruments
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Good wrapping technique visible, maintaining suture control

frame_analyses.description: Final knot tightening phase, instruments pulling in opposite directions
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Appropriate tensioning technique for securing knot

frame_analyses.description: Knot appears complete, instruments being withdrawn

knot_assessments.notes: Knot formation visible but specific throw sequence not clearly distinguishable

confidence_rationale: Video appears to be educational material showing knot tying technique rather than actual FLS task performance. No Penrose drain, target marks, or FLS trainer box visible. Cannot assess suture placement accuracy, drain closure, or specific throw sequence requirements.
cannot_determine: Actual suture placement through marked targets - no drain or marks visible
Deviation measurements - no reference marks present
Specific throw sequence (surgeon's knot vs single throws)
Whether this represents actual FLS task performance vs demonstration
technique_summary: Video shows skilled intracorporeal knot tying technique but does not appear to represent actual FLS Task 5 performance with required elements (Penrose drain, target marks, complete suturing sequence).
improvement_suggestions: Video does not show actual FLS task components for meaningful assessment
